error_signature: case sensitivity issues with table names
title: Case sensitivity issues with Supabase table names
symptom: When querying tables in Supabase, the user encounters errors due to case sensitivity in table names, leading to empty results or incorrect references.
applies_when: Using Supabase with mixed case table names
Querying tables that have foreign key relationships
not_this_if: All table names are consistently in lowercase
No foreign key relationships are involved
root_cause: Supabase treats table names as case-sensitive when they are created with mixed case. If a table is created with a capital letter, it must be referenced with the exact same casing in queries, otherwise, it defaults to lowercase, leading to mismatches.
fix_steps: Identify the correct case for all table names in your database
Update your SQL queries to match the exact case of the table names
Test the queries to ensure they return the expected results
agent_instruction: Ensure all table names in your SQL queries match the exact case used during their creation in Supabase.
